title: My Body Is Allowed to Change Shape
subtitle: An affirmation for the body that doesn't look the way it used to
excerpt: A short affirmation for the body that has changed: after children, in mid-life, after illness, or simply across the years. Rejects the premise that the body's job is to stay in one shape and affirms the body that is doing its work right now.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are struggling with a body that has changed shape and you are measuring it against an earlier version of itself.

content.text: A short affirmation for the body that has changed. Bodies change through pregnancy, illness, mid-life, age, medication, stress, and time. The cultural account of a body that holds one shape across decades is not a biological reality. This affirmation works with the body as it is.
Say it standing in front of a mirror, hand on your body somewhere. Three times, slowly.
My body is allowed to change shape. This body is doing its work right now.

The affirmation has two parts. The first is permission: the body is allowed to change. The second is a factual statement about what the body is doing, regardless of its shape. Both are true regardless of what the body looks like.
